description.text: Protocols: The cells were generated by infecting cells with 2nd generation lentiviruses. The genes are under Tet-on control. 2d and 4d indicates either doxycycline induction for 2 or 4 days. Het1A, Het1A-HNF4A and Het1A-GATA6 cells were cultured in DMEM (+10% FBS and 1% PenStrep) with 100ng/mL doxycycline for 2 or 4 days. Cells were trypsinised and counted. 50000 cells were taken forward for Tn5 reaction. 50000 cells were lysed in ATAC lysis buffer and Tn5 reactions were performed as described in Transposition of native chromatin for fast and sensitive epigenomic profiling of open chromatin, DNA-binding proteins and nucleosome position. 2013. Jason D. Buenrostro, Paul G. Giresi, Lisa C. Zaba, Howard Y. Chang and William J. Greenleaf. doi:10.1038/nmeth.2688 Libraries were constructed by amplifying transposed DNA with Illumina Nextera adapters.

description.text: Protocols: ATAC was performed following the protocol of Buenrostro et al. Briefly, 50,000 L3.6pl cells were trypsinized and washed twice with cold PBS. Then cells were re-suspended in lysis buffer (10mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 10mM NaCl, 3mM MgCl2, 0.1% IGEPAL CA-630) and incubated for 15 minutes at a rotating wheel at 4ºC followed by centrifugation and in re-suspended in 50 µl of transposition mix composed of 2.5µl of TDE1 (Nextera Tn5 Transposase), 25 µl TD (2x reaction buffer) in nuclease free water (Nextera DNA Library Prep Kit, FC-121-1030, Illumina). Then it was incubated at 37 degrees for 30 minutes. MinElute PCR Purification kit (Qiagen) was used for DNA extraction following the instructions of the manufacturer. ATAC libraries were made using the Nextera DNA Library Prep Kit according to Buenrostro et al. The libraries were amplified for 15 cycles in total of: 98ºC for 10 seconds, 63ºC for 30 seconds, and 72ºC for 60 seconds.

INSDC center name.text: WELLCOME TRUST SANGER INSTITUTE

description.text: Protocols: The spleen from a C57BL/6Jax mouse was mashed by a 2-ml syringe plunger through a 70 μm cell strainer (Fisher Scientific 10788201) into 30 ml 1X DPBS (ThermoFisher 14190169) supplied with 2 mM EDTA and 0.5% (w/v) BSA (Sigma A9418). Cells were centrifuged down, supernatant was removed, and the cell pellet was briefly vortexed. 5 ml 1X RBC lysis buffer (ThermoFisher 00-4300-54) was used to resuspend the cell pellet, and the cell suspension was vortexed again, and left on bench for 5 minutes to lyse red blood cells. Then 45 ml 1X DPBS was added, and cells were centrifuged down. 30 ml 1X DPBS were used to resuspend the cell pellet. The cell suspension was passed through a Miltenyi 30 μm Pre-Separation Filter (Miltenyi 130-041-407), and the cell number was determined using C-chip counting chamber (VWR DHC-N01). All centrifugations were done at 500 g, 4 °C, 5 minutes. After tagmentation, DAPI was added at a final concentration of 1 ug/ml. Then DAPI positive nuclei were sorted into 384-well plate using BD-INFLUX sorter. The nuclei sorting was performed at the Flow Cytometry facility at the Wellcome Trust Sanger Institute. Nuclei prepration and ATAC-seq procedure are performed simultaneously, i.e. Fast-ATAC protocol was used (Corces et al. Nature Genetics, 48, 1193-1203, 2016). Briefly, 50,000 cells were pelleted down at 500 g, 4 degree for 5 minutes. Then cell pellet was resuspended in 50 ul tagmentation mix containing 33 mM Tris-acetate, pH7.8, 66 mM potassium acetate, 10 mM magnesium acetate, 16% (v/v) dimethylformamide, 0.01% digitonin, 5 ul Tn5 transposes (Nextera kit, Illumina Cat No. FC-121-1030). The tagmentation reaction was put on a thermomixer, with 37 degree for 30 minutes. Then reaction was stopped by adding 50 ul 20 mM EDTA pH 8.0. After tagmentation, DAPI was added at a final concentration of 1 ug/ml. Then DAPI positive nuclei were sorted into 384-well plate using BD-INFLUX sorter. The nuclei sorting was performed at the Flow Cytometry facility at the Wellcome Trust Sanger Institute. DNA was purified first by Qiagen minElute PCR purification kit according to the manufacturer's instruction, then by AmupureXP beads with 0.5X upper cutoff and a 1.2X lower cutoff according to manufacturer's instruction. Libraries were constructed in the Illumina Nextera DNA Library preparation kit.

description.text: Protocols: 150000 Cells were lysed in ATAC-lysis buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.4, 10 mM NaCl, 3 mM MgCl2, 0.1% IGEPAL CA-630) and immediately processed for tagmentation. Tagmentation was performed as described in Buenrostro et al. Curr Protoc. Mol. Biol., 2016 using the Illumina Nextera Tn5 Transposase. ATAC-seq libraries were amplified from the tagmentation reaction by PCR using the PPC (PCR Primer mix) from the Illumina Nextera Tn5 Transposase kit and adding barcodes from the Nextera Index Kit (24 indexes 96 samples). DNA was purified using AmpPure XP beads.

description.text: Protocols: RS4:11 cells were electroporated alone (unedited, SPI1+/+) or with CAS9 and a guide RNA sequence (GGGGGUGGAAGUCCCAGUAA) targeting exon three of SPI1 (CRISPR Knockout Pool, Synthego). Editing efficiency approached 50%. Clonal populations of mono (SPI1+/-) and biallelically edited cells (SPI1-/-) were obtained via limiting dilution and clonal expansion. Edits were confirmed via Sanger sequencing and with PU.1 immunoblots. Cell aliquots of 100,000 were washed twice and then resuspended in cold lysis buffer before being spun down. The supernatants were discarded, and then nuclei were resuspended in cold transposition reaction mix from the Illumina Tagment DNA Enzyme kit. The transposition reaction was incubated at 37°C for 45 min. Immediately following transposition, 10µl of 3M Sodium Acetate was added to the DNA which was then purified using a Qiagen MinElute Kit. Transposed DNA fragments were PCR amplified for 12 cycles using NEBNext High-Fidelity 2x PCR Master Mix(NEB) with Nextera primers (Illumina). The PCR reaction was cleaned up using AMPureXP beads (Agencourt)

description.text: Protocols: Naïve T-cells were isolated from peripheral blood mononuclear cells (PBMCs) by magnetic bead separation using a Naive CD4+ T Cell Isolation Kit II (Miltenyi Biotec). The PBMCs were isolated from the buffy coat fraction of human blood using phase separation over lymphoprep (Axis Shields). Stimulation towards Th1 differentiation for 0.5, 1, 2, 6 and 24h. Specifically the cells were treated with a 1/1 ratio of Dynabeads® Human T-Activator CD3/CD28 (Thermofisher), recombinant IL-2 (10 ng/ml), recombinant IL-12 (5 ng/ml) and anti-IFNG (MAB285) (5 µg/ml) (Rnd Systems) in RPMI 1640 media (Gibco). For the Progesterone treated samples: Sampels were pre-incubated with 50 µM Progesterone (P4) for 12-14h prior to experiment and then reconstituted with fresh media containing above 50 µM P4 and above mentioned TH1 stimulation reagetnts to start the reaction. Non-progeterone treated sample were pre-incubated and reconstituted in growth media without P4. DNA was extracted from 50000 cells per sample in accordance with the OMNI-ATAC-seq method. Corces et al. Nature Methods 2017 Libarary construction was carried out in accordance with the OMNI-ATAC-seq method. Corces et al. Nature Methods 2017. Illumina NEXTERA primers and sequencing adapters were used to construct the index.

description.text: Protocols: The following cells were sorted from human children healthy donor tonsils: naive CD4+CD45RO- T cells (Naive) and CD4+CD45RO+CD25-CXCR5hiPD1hi follicular helper CD4+ T cells (TFH). For each of the TFH and Naive libraries, 50,000 live cells were harvested. The ATAC-seq libraries were generated following a previously published protocol (Buenrostro et al., Nature Methods, 2013). The nuclei were isolated from the 50,000 cells. The isolated nuclei were treated with Tn5 Transposes from Nextera DNA Sample Preparation Kit and the transposed DNA was purified using a Qiagen MinElute Kit. The transposed DNA libraries were amplified by PCR for 12 cycles and isolated by Ampure XP beads.

prompt.content: You are a smart curator of biological data.

I will input JSON formatted metadata of a sample for a biological experiment.
Your task is to extract relevant biological information (if present) from the input data and format it according to the specified schema.

---
Categories to extract:
- "cell_line (single value)":
  - Cell line is a group of cells that are genetically identical and have been cultured in a laboratory setting. e.g., HeLa, Jurkat, HEK293, etc.
- "cell_type (single value)":
  - Cell type is a classification of cells based on their structure and function, representing normal cells. e.g., neuron, hepatocyte, etc.
- "tissue (single value)":
  - Tissue is a group of cells that work together to perform a specific function in an organism. e.g., lung, liver, heart, etc.
- "disease (single value)":
  - Disease is an abnormal state of the body or its parts that impairs normal function. e.g., lung cancer, down syndrome, etc.
- "drug (multiple values (array))":
  - Drug is a chemical or biological substance used to prevent, diagnose, treat, or alleviate disease or symptoms, or to modify physiological functions. e.g., aspirin, metformin, trastuzumab, etc.

---
Output rules:
  - Return only JSON, matching the provided schema (via the `format` option).
  - For categories with `string` value_type:
      - If you can identify a value, output a single concise canonical name (not a free-form description).
      - If no value can be found, output null.
  - For categories with `array` value_type:
      - If you can identify one or more values, output an array of concise canonical names.
      - If no values can be found, output null (not an empty array).
  - Prefer exact mentions in the input; if multiple candidates exist, pick the most specific and widely recognized.
  - Do not hallucinate or infer values that are absent from the input.
  - Do not mix different kinds of concepts in the same category.

---
Here is the input metadata:

message.thinking:
